Title: 1a - Introduction To Seedlab
Content: This module provides an introduction to Seedlab Australia and an overview of how the course works.

Title: 2 - Consumers & Competition
Content: Understanding your consumers, customers and where you sit with your competitors is a vital first step in knowing your business and being able to make informed choices. The content within this course will help you focus on these market insights.

Title: Week 2 - Your Business
Content: Having a sound understanding of your business fundamentals is essential to operating a profitable and sustainable organisation. The topics within this course aim to remind you of some of these fundamentals and even stimulate you to consider aspects you may not have considered before.

Title: Week 3 - Your Product & Who Buys It
Financials 101
Content: This course contains content provide by Chartered Accountants Australia and New Zealand through their support for Seedlab Australia.

Title: Woolworths Seedlab Expo
Content:
This module is the 'go to place' for all of the information related to the Woolworths Seedlab Expo.

The date of the next Expo is Wednesday 22nd April 2026 (EXPO 7)

Title: The Seedlab Hub
Content:
The Hub is your portal to glory! This is the place to showcase your retail ready products – their pics, unique selling points, your capacity and your prices and more. You can add your business pitch in video and written/visual format to add further information, and to inspire. You can also showcase innovative new products you may have in development, with timelines.

The next step the Category Manger will take is to reach out to you for more information, perhaps asking you to send in samples, or make a time to chat.

Note: The Hub DOES NOT replace engaging in the normal Category Review Process, nor does it replace uploading your product information via Partner Hub, which we recommend you also do.

However, the Seedlab Hub may supercharge you as a potential new Woolworths Supplier. We hope so, anyway! Good luck!

To go to the Seedlab Hub click this link:- https://seedlabhub.com.au/login.

Title: Week 1 - The Seedlab Australia Bootcamp Program and You
Content: This module provides an introduction to Seedlab Australia, an overview of how the course works, and what to prepare for Week 1.

Title: Goal Setting For Success
Content: In this module Dr Hazel MacTavish-West discusses the importance of setting short, medium, and long term goals to ensure that you end out where you want to be. Some tools and templates are then presented to help you undertake this activity. These templates and accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Title: What Style of Business Are You?
Content: In this video Ray Butcher outlines some of the major business types and structures. He then describes how the objectives of these businesses may differ and the trajectory that a business may follow as it grows and matures. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Title: Performing a SWOT Analysis
Content:
Amanda Hinds takes you through SWOT as a tool for your business, and how to perform a SWOT analysis.

Tags: SWOT, Amanda, swot analysis, strengths, weaknesses, threats, opportunities, business tool

Title: The Importance of Setting Specifications
Content: In this video Ray Butcher describes the importance of creating documented specifications for all ingredients and other inputs to your process as well as defining a specification for your outputs to communicate with your customer. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab. The homework from this session (referred to as worksheet #5) forms part of the upload for your Cultivate pitch.

Title: A Basic Business Checklist
Content:
Please watch this video or read the slides, and complete a Business Checklist for your business.

The Accompanying slides and homework worksheet (#4) can be found under the 'Materials' tab. The checklist is 'essential worksheet #4' which we require you to complete and upload with your pitch at the end of bootcamp if you wish to be considered for Cultivate.

Title: Your Business
Content: In this video Ray Butcher provides a grounding in the basics of business, metrics and understanding business finance. These are skills that are essential to have when running a business and this introduction is intended to act as a reminder to the experienced practitioner or as a starting point for a less developed business operator.

Title: The Seedlab Business Planner
Content:
The Seedlab Business Planner is a version of the popular LEAN startup plan used to guide and inform startup businesses around the world. The lessons presented throughout the Seedlab Australia program will take you through the key areas of the plan and offer instruction as to how to populate your business plan on a page.

In this video Dr Hazel MacTavish-West introduces the planner and describes how we use it. Various formats of the planner template can be found under the 'Materials' tab for download.

The planner is designed to be printed out and used as a live document, often using sticky notes to allow rapid changes and thought experiments. A possible alternative to using a hard copy is to use an image of the planner as a background in your favourite virtual whiteboard or planning application.

Below is an example of such a plan being used in Jamboard which is a Google suite application available online (jamboard.google.com) but many other applications could be used (e.g. Trello, Miro etc.).

The Seedlab Business Planner is adapted from The Business Model Canvas and the Lean Canvas created by Ash Maurya and is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-Share Alike 3.0 Un-ported License.

Title: What Are You?
Content: In this module Dr Hazel MacTavish-West challenges you to try to describe who you are and what your product is in a simple and succinct way. This is an essential stepping stone on the path to being able to communicate with your markets. The associated pre-work sheet can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Title: Seedlab Overview
Content: In this module Dr Hazel MacTavish-West provides an overview of Seedlab Australia, its programs, and specifically how the Bootcamp program operates.

Title: Meet The Team
Content: In the modules below you can meet the Seedlab Australia team who will be providing the content and information throughout bootcamp.

Title: Zoom Like An Expert
Content: In this video Darren West provides a brief introduction to how we use Zoom in Seedlab Australia as well as demonstrating some of the basic functionality.

Title: Watch Me Eat
Content: In addition to talking to consumers, a powerful technique to help understand their problems is to observe their experiences. By experience observation it is often possible to identify problems and pain points that consumer may not even consciously register. An example of this process is given through an exercise called ‘Watch me eat’ where critical observation is coupled with a powerful questioning method called TED (Tell, Explain and Describe). In this video Dr Hazel MacTavish-West describes how to use this process to maximum effect. The associated worksheet and slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Title: The Target Consumer Persona
Content: The Target Consumer Persona can be a powerful tool tool to help you capture and understand the essence of your target consumer and assist with internal communication of this profile and ensuring that all of your activities are aligned to target (or targets). In this video Andy Cooley and Iain White introduce the process of developing a consumer persona. The accompanying slides and associated worksheet can be found under the 'Materials' tab. The Target Consumer Persona is 'essential worksheet #2' which we require you to complete and upload with your pitch at the end of bootcamp if you wish to be considered for Cultivate.

Title: Understanding the Customer and Consumer
Content: In this video Ray Butcher considers the differences in needs between customers and consumers. These differing needs are then explored through the various routes to market that are available for FMCG. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Title: Ian Sypkes from Sanyou Baijiu
Content:
In this short video Ian Sypkes from Sanyou Baijiu talks about his experiences from having taken part in the Seedlab Tasmania program.

Also available as audio only.

Title: Introduction to the Seedlab Australia Academy
Content: The short video below provides a brief tour of the Seedlab Australia Academy learning portal.

Title: What is a Minimum Viable Product?
Content: In this session Darren West introduces the concept of the Minimum Viable Product (MVP) and discusses why they can be useful in developing new ideas through the idea often referred to as 'failing fast'. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Title: Production and Process Flow
Content: In this video Ray Butcher discusses the importance of having a solid understanding of your production and process flows. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Title: LEAN and Costings Review
Content: In this video Ray Butcher discusses the benefits of adopting a LEAN strategy for your business ensuring a culture of continuous improvement. The video finishes with a revisit to product costings in the light of consideration of processes and LEAN. The accompanying slides and the costing worksheet (worksheet #6) can be found under the 'Materials' tab. The worksheet is required to be uploaded as part of your Cultivate pitch.

Title: What Does Retail Ready Involve?
Content: In this video Ray Butcher discusses the things that you need to consider to become retail ready both in terms or product and organisation. The accompanying slides and the Retail Ready Checklist template can be found under the 'Materials' tab. The worksheet is required to be uploaded as part of your Cultivate pitch.

Title: Capacity and Capability
Content:
In this video Ray Butcher considers the differences between capacity and capability, and then demonstrates why a clear understanding of these features of your own business must inform you decisions to supply to retail.

The accompanying slides and the Capacity Worksheet can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Title: Andy Jackman from Red Cow Organics
Content:
In this short video Andy Jackman from Red Cow Organics talks about her experiences from having taken part in the Seedlab Tasmania program and how business has developed subsequently.

Also available as audio only.

Title: Your Brand Story
Content: In this video Andy Cooley and Iain White introduce the power of the brand story as a vehicle with which to inform and frame communication about your brand to your consumers. The accompanying slide set can be found under the 'Materials' tab along with a worksheet template to help you develop your brand story. This worksheet is the required worksheet #9 to be uploaded with your Cultivate pitch.

Title: Food Regulation: Getting Your Labelling Right
Content: This module, presented by Fiona Fleming from the AIFST (Australian Institute of Food Science and Technology) discusses the issues around food regulations and ensuring that your labels are correct and compliant.

Title: A Beginners Guide to Top-Notch Visual Content
Content: In this video Jericho Cleary from Digital Dandy talks about using your iPhone or similar to take great videos and photos. Getting the lighting right. Using the timer. Portrait or Landscape? Use these 10 tips and your photos and videos will sing!

Title: So You Want More? Pitch to Cultivate
Content:
As Bootcamp draws to an end, what happens next?

In this short video Dr. Hazel MacTavish-West describes what the Seedlab Australia Cultivate program looks like and provides further information around the application process and deadlines for the program. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Title: Pitch Perfection
Content:
This module provides information on how to craft that perfect pitch for any occasion. Accompanying materials can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

One of the most important things when preparing for a pitch is planning. Depending on the purpose of the pitch the plan will need to be adapted to suit, but a useful canvas that we have used can be found here at www.pitchplanner.com (external site).

Title: The People Stuff- Knowing Yourself
Content: In this short video Amanda Hinds discusses the importance of understanding your and your teams different strengths, weaknesses and motivators. Amanda then challenges us to take a personality test to help improve this self knowledge. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Title: The Vegdoctor's 13P's for Presenting Perfectly
Content: In this session Dr. Hazel MacTavish-West (aka the VegDoctor) shares her experiences of presenting publicly and distills these thoughts into her 13 P’s of Presenting Perfectly. The accompanying slides can be found under the ‘Materials’ tab.

Title: Mike Wallas - Raising Capital
Content:
In this video Mike Wallas shares his experiences as a serial investor about what investors expect and how to go about raising capital.

Also available as audio only.

Title: SWOT CIP and GAP with Ray Butcher
Content:
In this video Ray Butcher introduces some powerful tools that can be used to analyse your businesses and processes. When combined together these techniques help foster a culture of continuous improvement and LEAN operation. These concepts build upon some of those introduced in some Bootcamp sessions. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Keywords: Ray Butcher, SWOT, Strength Weaknesses Opportunities Threats, Continuous Improvement, Gap Analysis, LEAN

Title: Business Financials 101 with Stacey Price
Content:
In this recording financial coach Stacey Price from Healthy Business Finances discusses a broad range of issues related to basic business financials. Aspects covered include record keeping to make life easier for you and your accountant, how to spot when something has gone wrong and some general hints and tips based on a wealth of real-world experience.

Keywords: Financials, Reports, Stacey Price, Accounts

Title: LEAN Culture with Ray Butcher
Content:
In this video Ray Butcher discusses the benefits of developing a LEAN culture for your business ensuring a philosophy of continuous improvement and waste minimisation. Ray leads us through the principles behind LEAN and shares some of his own personal LEAN journey. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Keywords: Ray Butcher, LEAN Culture, Continuous Improvement, Waste Minimisation

Title: Food Trends- presented by Bazil Stander 2021
Content:
In this session, Executive Innovation Chef Lead Bazil Stander describes his observations of current food trends, especially in the plant based arena, as a result of market innovation research undertaken by Woolworths. The session was introduced by Dr. Hazel MacTavish-West and Jason McQuaid, General Manager of Chilled, Bakery and Deli with Woolworths.

Keywords: Bazil Stander, Woolworths, Trends, Innovation, Plant Based

Title: The Innovation Process- presented by Eva Wildsperger and Verity Jackson
Content:
In this session Eva Wildsperger, General Manager of FoodCo. Fresh and Verity Jackson, Own Brand Manager of Fresh Prepared, Food Co. provide an insight to the FoodCo. development process and the various gates used to control specification, development, launch and review of products.

Key Words: Woolworths, Innovation Process, Pipeline, Launch, Eva Wildserger, Verity Jackson

Title: Getting Listed and Quantium- presented by Kiri Ramsay
Content:
In this session Kiri Ramsay, a category manager with Woolworths describes the process of getting listed with Woolworths and the importance of using data such as Quantium to inform product and NPD decisions. Quantium is consumer data generated through the Woolworths store and online sales systems and provides rich consumer data to assist marketing decisions. The session was introduced by Dr. Hazel MacTavish-West and Jason McQuaid. The accompanying slides can be found under the ‘Materials’ tab.

Keywords: Woolworths, Range Review, Listing, Quantium , Market Insights, Kiri Ramsay, Data Insights

Title: Introduction to Cartology- presented by Tom Small
Content:
In this session Tom Small, a Group Client Partnership Manager with Cartology describes how data can be used to enhance the customer journey of engagement and hopefully improve sales outcomes through utilising multiple channels.  The accompanying slides can be found under the ‘Materials’ tab.

Keywords: Woolworths, Cartology, Customer Engagement, Data, Tom Small

Title: Getting Your Labeling Right with Fiona Fleming
Content:
This module, presented by Fiona Fleming from the AIFST (Australian Institute of Food Science and Technology) discusses the issues around food regulations and ensuring that your labels are correct and compliant. Note: this is a repeat of the module provided in Bootcamp but is repeated here for access convenience.

Keywords: Fiona Fleming, AIFST, FSANZ, Label Regulations, Compliance

Title: Getting Your Health Claims Right with Fiona Fleming
Content:
This module, presented by Fiona Fleming from the AIFST (Australian Institute of Food Science and Technology) discusses the issues around food regulations and ensuring that your labels are correct and compliant with regards to allowable and legal health claims that may (or may not) be able to be made about your products.

Keywords: Fiona Fleming, AIFST, Regulation, Health Claim, FSANZ

Title: Shelf Life Trial Design with Jasmine Lacis-Lee
Content:
This module, presented by Jasmine Lacis-Lee from BVAQ introduces the processes involved in designing meaningful shelf life trials. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

This content is also available in audio only

Keywords: Shelf Life Trial, BVAQ, Jasmine Lacis-Lee, Microbilogy, Spoilage, Trial Design, Food Safety Testing

Title: Food and Drink Trends 2021 with Dr Hazel MacTavish-West
Content:
In this session Dr Hazel MacTavish-West gives a rapid-fire insight into what she sees as the major food and drink trends for 2021 and beyond.

Keywords: Hazel MacTavish-West, Food Trends, Market Insight

Title: Documenting Procedures and Protocols with Rob Munton
Content:
Rob Munton from Food Projx Pty Ltd discusses the importance of documenting procedures and processes in food production. Documentation is an essential component in being able to produce safe, repeatable, consistent and high quality products in a controlled, verifiable and audit-able manner. Although the examples used are primarily food focused the principles extend to any production environment and process. Associated worksheets and further information can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Keywords: Rob Munton, Food Projx, Documentation, Standard Operating Procedures, SOP, Protocol, Document Control

Title: Production Scale-up with Rob Munton
Content:
Rob Munton from Food Projx Pty Ltd discusses the challenges of production scale-up within a food production process and how to manage this. The focus of the discussion is around some of the considerations and thought processes that need to be applied in planning and executing the scale-up activity within a business planning context. Associated worksheets and further information can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Keywords: Rob Munton, Food Projx, Scale-up, Factory Planning, Process Design, Risk Analysis, Risk Matrix

Title: Legal and Intellectual Property Considerations with Davies Collison Cave
Content: This series of modules have been recorded by Davies Collison Cave to provide an introduction to some of the aspects of intellectual property law and general business law that are becoming more important in the highly globalised and connected business world. To obtain the best from these modules it is suggested that they are watched in the order that they are presented here.

Title: Drivers for Value Adding Foods with Dr Hazel MacTavish-West
Content:
In this session Dr Hazel MacTavish-West discusses the potential benefits of adding value to food and beverage products, and considers some of the ways that this can be approached from a consumer driver perspective. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Keywords: Value Adding, Hazel MacTavish-West, Carrots, Consumer Drivers, Value

Title: Business Planning with Ray Butcher
Content:
In this video Ray Butcher discusses some important aspects of business planning that can help your business directly but also help demonstrate your ability to deliver to your customers. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Keywords: Planning, Ray, Butcher, Capacity, Plans, Business, Strategic, Organization

Title: Capability, Capacity and Continuity of Supply with Ray Butcher
Content:
In this video Ray Butcher discusses the importance of being able to understand, measure and communicate your business capability and capacity in order to ensure continuity of supply. Several worked examples are provided and the viewer is recommended to reference the accompanying slides under the 'Materials' tab.

Keywords: Capacity, Capability, Continuity, Supply, Ray, Butcher, Production, Shift, Line

Title: Keeping Control of the Value Chain with Ray Butcher
Content:
In this video Ray Butcher considers what the value chain is and how it needs to be controlled as part of the decision to value-add products to reduce risk and improve returns on investment. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Keywords: Ray Butcher, Value Chain, Value-add

Title: Food Microbiology 101 with Jasmine Lacis-Lee
Content:
Jasmine Lacis-Lee from BVAQ presents this module about the basics of food microbiology. The module discusses some of the causes and mechanisms of food spoilage which are essential to understand in the context of food safety. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

The session is also available as audio only,

Keywords: BVAQ, Jasmine Lacis-Lee, Spoilage, Safety, Microbiology

Title: Food Allergen Labelling with Jasmine Lacis-Lee
Content:
Presented by Jasmine Lacis-Lee from BVAQ this module discusses the importance of identifying allergens in your products and the importance of correct labelling to ensure the safety of your consumers. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

This module is also available as audio only.

KEYWORDS: BVAQ, Jasmine Lacis-Lee, Allergen, Label, Safety

Title: Control Your Cashflow with BDO
Content:
In this module Svetlana Kolyasnikova and Marc Orchard from BDO discuss the importance of cashflow, especially during early or growth stages of a business.

KEYWORDS: BDO, Cash Flow, Svetlana Kolyasnikova , Marc Orchard, Cashflow, Growth, Funding

Title: Competitor Analysis with Ray Butcher
Content:
This short video describes the importance of competitor analysis in understanding your market segment and provides some guidance about how to perform such an analysis. The accompanying slides and template can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

KEYWORDS: Ray Butcher, competitor analysis, WIBIT, market, segment

Title: Retail Views on the Value Chain- presented by Jason McQuaid and Serena Anson-Cope
Content:
In this video Jason McQuaid and Serena Anson-Cope from Woolworths provide an insight to the national retailer view of the value chain in the value added grocery sector and also share some views of how this sector may develop in the future. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

KEYWORDS: Woolworths, value chain, UP stores, premium, value, economy, Jason McQuaid, Serena Anson-Cope, value add

Title: Dealing with Retailers- presented by Ben Ewens
Content:
Dealing with retailers can be a time-consuming yet essential part of growing your business and expanding your potential distribution network. In this video Ben Ewens shares his extensive experiences in dealing with retailers in the FMCG category and describes some techniques that have proved successful. It is important to remember that dealings with retailers need to be undertaken with sensitivity and acknowledgement that all parties are busy and have their own desired outcomes and a professional approach is an absolute must.

KEYWORDS: Ben Ewens, samples, LinkedIn, research, buyer, contact

Title: DISCover Your Behaviour Style with Sally Fairless and Amanda Hinds
Content:
In this session, presented as part of hangout 2 for Cultivate cohort 1, Sally Fairless and Amanda Hinds introduce the DISC behaviour profiling tool as a vehicle for better understanding yourself, your team and the people that you interact with. The associated slides can be found under the 'Materials. tab. A version of the DISC test can be found here. (external link)

KEYWORDS: DISC, profile, personality, Sally Fairless, Amanda Hinds. behaviour

Title: Woolworths Digital Update 2022 with Helen Cross
Content: In this video, Helen Cross, Head of Digital Merchandising for Fresh Foods with WooliesX discusses how digital is becoming a more important component of the retail experience and highlights some considerations and opportunities to be considered to maximise your market potential.

Title: Working with Distributors- presented by Ben Ewens
Content:
Working with distributors can be an efficient and quick way of expanding your potential distribution network and increasing your product reach. In this video Ben Ewens shares his extensive experiences in dealing with retailers in the FMCG category and describes some techniques that have proved successful. An important aspect of this can be understanding the desired outcomes of the distribution relationship from all parties.

This content is also available as audio only.

Keywords: Ben Ewens, distributor, agreement, distribution, network, territory

Title: The Innovation Pipeline- Idea Generation with Penny Bloss
Content:
In this module, Penny Bloss introduces the concept of the Innovation Pipeline to help undertake innovation and idea generation in a structured way with outcomes that can be meaningfully evaluated. Penny describes practical approaches that can be used to help stimulate new ideas and solutions in you business. Other tips and tricks discussed are how to bring your team with you on the innovation journey since innovation is often best played as a team sport.

This has been updated in 2025.

The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Keywords: Penny Bloss, Innovation, ideation, teamwork, SCAMPER, pipeline, brainstorm, process, hack

Title: Working with Woolworths, an Overview- presented by Brendan Gray
Content:
In this recording Brendan Gray, Head of Support Services for Woolworths provides an overview of the process of engaging with Woolworths as a supplier and some of the mechanisms behind the scenes. The accompanying slides can be found under the ‘Materials’ tab.

Note: This session was recorded in 2020 so any mention of Dan Murphys process may no longer be up-to-date.

Key words: Woolworths, Brendan Gray, process, purchasing

Title: The Innovation Pipeline- When, How and Why with Penny Bloss
Content:
In this module, Penny Bloss continues the discussion around the Innovation Pipeline and describes when, how and why we may need to innovate. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

This was updated for 2025.

Key words: Innovation pipeline, Penny Bloss, process, fad, trend, sweet spot

Title: Getting Listed with Woolworths 2020- presented by Kiri Ramsay
Content:
This is the first of three recordings from the initial Seedlab Tasmania Woolworths information session held on the 21st August 2020. In this recording Kiri Ramsay, a category manager with Woolworths describes the process of listing with the company and some suggestions as to how best approach the relationship. The slide set can be accessed from under the ‘Materials’ tab.

Key words: Kiri Ramsay, category manager, product listing, review

Title: Building a Brand and Working With Woolworths- Lyn Radford from Chobani
Content:
In this second recording from the initial Seedlab Tasmania Woolworths information session from the 21st August 2020 Lyn Radford from Chobani shares her experience of developing a new brand in Australia and engaging with a large retailer like Woolworths. The accompanying slides can be accessed from under the ‘Materials’ tab.

Key words: Lyn Radford, Chobani, brand development, relationship, plan

Title: Navigating the Woolworths Supply Chain- presented by Lang Juckes
Content:
In this presentation recorded on the 28th August 2020, Lang Juckes, head of Supply Planning from Woolworths provides and overview of the Woolworths supply chain and supply chain planning operations with particular emphasis on the relationship with the supplier. Accompanying information can be found under the ‘Materials’ tab.

Key words: Lang Juckes, distribution, order fulfillment, logistics, planning, stock, supply

Title: Ensuring Product Quality and Safety- presented by Sarah Winwright
Content:
In this presentation recorded on the 28th August 2020 Sarah Winwright, Quality Manager for Woolworths FoodCo provides an overview of the steps that must be taken to ensure that food products are safe, trusted by the customer and meet the customer expectation every time. Accompanying information can be found under the ‘Materials’ tab.

Key words: Sarah Winwright, HACCP, safety, quality, process, trust

Title: Document Wrangling for Beginners
Content:
Document control is an essential part of managing the information within your business. This short video introduces some of the aims, requirements and possible solutions for a basic document control regime. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Key words: Document control, backup, storage, approval, signoff, filename, records, file

Title: The Innovation Pipeline- Testing the Market with Penny Bloss
Content:
In this module, Penny Bloss continues the discussion around the Innovation Pipeline and describes how to test your ideas in the real market to validate your concepts and 'fail fast'. Techniques for consumer engagement are introduced and the practical application of the Minimum Viable Product (MVP) is revisited.

Key words: Innovation pipeline, Penny Bloss, interview, survey, MVP, minimum viable product, testing, fail fast

Title: The Bootcamp Worksheets - All In One Place
Content:
Throughout Bootcamp there are various tasks that you will be asked to complete prior to Live Discussions.

Head to the Materials tab, and you'll find them all in one place!

Some of these are designed to help develop your own understanding and skills, some are designed to help us to understand what stage your business is currently at and what additional help may be required.

Title: Visual Identity- presented by The Offices
Invoicing with Stacey Price
Content:
In this video Stacey Price from Healthy Business Finances provides an introduction to Invoicing which is a vital activity for any commercial enterprise yet something that is handled so poorly by many, and which can have significant implications for taxation and cashflow. This content is supported by Chartered Accountants of Australia and New Zealand.

This content is also available as audio only.

Key words: CAANZ, Stacey Price, invoicing, cashflow, cash, GST, debt, payment, terms, contract

Title: GST with Stacey Price
Content:
In this video Stacey Price from Healthy Business Finances provides an introduction to GST, the understanding of which is an essential part of running a business in Australia yet is often a cause of great confusion. The content addresses subjects such as when to register, when you have to register, when to charge GST, when not to charge GST and a reminder that GST is not your money! This content is supported by Chartered Accountants of Australia and New Zealand.

Key words: CAANZ, Stacey Price, GST, ATO, tax, invoice

Title: Managing Your Time with Sally Fairless
Content:
In this short module Sally Fairless introduces some techniques to help prioritise your time objectively based on the likely impact of the tasks on your business objectives. This helps remove the emotional or personal bias that can cause us to focus on the 'fun' things but not necessarily the 'critical' things. A worksheet and the accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Key words: Time management, Sally Fairless, task, priority, rock, sand

Title: An Introduction to Artisans Bend- presented by Sam Penny
Content:
Sam Penny of Artisans Bend introduces their market place on-line portal as a potential route to market, along with the new Artisan Send program which has the potential to offer mainland based warehousing, order pick and dispatch through a logistics partner operating a fleet of refrigerated vehicles.

Key words: Artisans Bend, Sam Penny, logistics, pick, pack, send, warehouse

Title: Human Resource Management with Amanda Hinds
Content:
Amanda Hinds discusses some of the aspects of Human Resource Management that need to be considered as you recruit staff and expand your business. While it can be a big step, with careful planning and execution it can be achieved with the minimum of dramas. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Key Words: Amanda Hinds, Human Resource Management, recruiting, employment, staff, harassment, discrimination, performance, induction, safety

Title: Interviewing Staff with Amanda Hinds
Content:
In this short presentation prepared by Amanda Hinds a few basic reminders are shared to help you interview well and reduce stress all round. The accompanying slides with templates can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Key words: Amanda Hinds, interview, recruitment

Title: Performance Management with Amanda Hinds
Content:
In this short presentation prepared by Amanda Hinds a few basic reminders are shared to help you manage staff performance to help achieve a happy and high performing organisation. The accompanying slides and template can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Key words: Amanda Hinds, performance management, high performing, under performing, review

Title: Recruiting Staff with Amanda Hinds
Content:
In this short presentation prepared by Amanda Hinds a few basic reminders are shared to help you plan your recruiting process for the best results. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Key words: Amanda Hinds, staff, recruiting, interview, advertise

Title: Investor Readiness with Ian Mason
Content:
In this session Ian Mason from Humble discusses the how, when and most importantly why of raising capital and investment. The benefits and pitfalls are considered along with what you need to to to be prepared to undertake the process successfully. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Key words: Investor, capital, raising, Ian Mason,

Title: Working With Journalists with Ian Mason
Content:
Ian Mason from Humble describes how to use journalists and media to help get your story out there and provides some rules and guidelines to improve the chance of engagement. The accompanying slides can be found under the ‘Materials’ tab.

Key words: Ian Mason, journalist, press release, media, pr, messaging

Title: International Market Entry with Ian Mason
Content:
Ian Mason from Humble provides insights into exploring international markets for your products and highlights the benefits, the risks and the challenges of following this path so that you can prepare well and de-risk the exercise. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Key words: Ian Mason, International, export, market, risk, sales

Title: Digital Marketing- presented by Kings Design
Pitching Your Products
Content:
In this module Dr Hazel MacTavish-West and Ray Butcher lead you through the process of pitching your products to retailers and provide a wealth of insights and tips based on their experiences. Title: Creating a Business Plan
Content:
Congratulations! By now, you should (hopefully) have your Seedlab Business Startup Plan completed (as in filled in, remember it is a living document that is never totally complete).

With this one page document you can now encapsulate the key points important to defining, describing and communicating your business both internally and to others. However, in some instances this high level detail will require expansion with further information, such as when trying to attract investment, partners or collaborators, and it is at this point the full business plan becomes useful.

For many startups creating a full business plan can seem a daunting task, but you are lucky in that you have already done a lot of the hard work in generating your startup plan. Armed with this document, and a number of other resources given below the creation of a full business plan should be no more than a ‘fill in the blanks’ exercise with information you have previously documented.

The federal government provide a range of information and tutorials, these resources are available through the portal business.gov.au and in particular for business plans this LINK (link checked 6th March 2022) where a step-by-step process is provided to guide the user through the business plan template that can be downloaded at the bottom of the page.

Title: 1 - Business And Leadership
Content:
The Business and Leadership masterclass was held on the 31st March 2022 and was hosted by Sally Fairless. The presenters provided a wide and varied conversation around different aspects of leadership including defining purpose (Kat Dunn), the need for planned and clear communication (Duncan Robinson) and some practical examples of core business principles (Ray Butcher). There are also two pre-recorded sessions from Jason Blanchard discussing retail strategy and growth (which are available below).

Jason Blanchard: Retail Strategy

Jason Blanchard: A Story About Growth

The video from the live session can be viewed below.

Program and speaker introductions:

Talk 1: Purpose Driven Work

Kat Dunn is a humanitarian capitalist with a passion for scaling social ventures and redesigning capitalism to benefit all stakeholders and the planet. She was the CEO of Grameen Australia, a social business inspired by the work of Nobel Peace Prize Winner, founder of Grameen Bank, Professor Muhammad Yunus. Kat is also a Board member for Food Frontier – the industry accelerator and think tank for the alternative protein industry (plant-based meat and clean meat).

Talk 2: Retail Leadership & Scaling Growth

Jason Blanchard is the former Chief Operating Officer of Blooms Chemist and has previously worked at Priceline Pharmacy, Best & Less and Woolworths. Jason will share his journey from playing state sport to being a highly sought-after Senior Executive. During his time at Blooms, Jason recruited and scaled the National Operations Team to 9 direct reports and 1,024 retail staff across 109 stores.

Talk 3: Getting Your Message Across

For almost 5 years Duncan Robinson hosted a breakfast show in Sydney. It’s ok, most people have no idea who Duncan is either. He was on Australia’s largest community radio station 103.2FM doing the breakfast show from 2016 - 2021. Duncan will share how the team grew the active listener base three-fold and his lessons on how to get your message across to over a 1 million daily active listeners (his customers).

Talk 4: The Long Road To Success

Ray Butcher has been involved in the retail of food since age eight serving in his aunt’s corner grocer in St Albans just outside London. He has worked for Tesco, Safeway and Woolworths. Currently, Ray is a Non- Executive Director in multiple businesses including Australian Garlic Producers and Houstons Farm fresh salads, He provides business wisdom to the Executive Teams guiding the management of the businesses, sales and marketing teams and QA teams.

Title: The Product Information Form
Content:
The Product Information Form (PIF) has been developed as a tool by the Australian and New Zealand food industry to collect and share product information that is needed to demonstrate compliance with various obligations under regulatory requirements or industry codes. The Australian Food and Grocery Council is responsible for managing the scheme and a link to the current user documentation for the system can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

There are a number of different certification or accreditation schemes depending on the product and/or ingredient and the Product Information User Guide provides some additional guidance aroud this.

The Product Information User Guide – section 6.3 lists a range of certification and endorsement programs. 

This list may not be complete but gives you an idea.

There will also be ISO/AS standards that companies can use, for example:

ISO 23662:2021- Definitions and technical criteria for foods and food ingredients suitable for vegetarians or vegans and for labelling and claims

Title: People and Personalities
Content:
With roots in the theories developed by Myers-Briggs the 16 personality types are based on a collection of congnitive functions which impact all aspects of life through how we process information and how we make decisions.

Having an understanding of where your personality type sits within this framework can be a useful starting point for being more aware of how you operate and how you interact with others around you, and can help you develop better strategies for for approaching challenges both in business and personal life.

There are several on-line resources that can be used to take the 16 personality test, including this site (please note, the link will take you to an external site):- 16personalities.com.

Amanda Hinds describes a little more about the 16 personalities in the video below, and the accompanying slides can be found under the ‘Materials’ tab.

Title: Australian Recycling Label Resources
Content:
The ability to reduce and recycle packaging is becoming ever more impotant in the retail landscape, especially when considering the 2025 National Packaging Targets of 100% of packaging to be reusable, recyclable or compostable, 70% of plastic packaging recycled or composted, 50% average recycled content across all packaging and phasing out of problematic and unecessary single-use plastic packaging.

The Australian Packaging Covenant Organisation (APCO) is a not for profit organisation that is leading the development of a circular economy for Australia and in partnership with Planet Ark has developed the Australian Recycling Label (ARL) Program which is rapidly being adopted by major retail operations across Australia and New Zealand. The ARL program leads to a set of recycling information symbols for the seperate components of the packaging with the classifications determined through an evidence based methodology.

A program has been launched specifically to assist SME businesses to evaluate and adopt the ARL scheme called the 'ARL Marketplace'.

A number of useful links for APCO, the ARL and ARL marketplace can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Key Words: APCO, recycling, plastic, ARL

Title: EXPO FAQ
Content:
What questions do you have? Email hazel@seedlabaustralia.com.au

Question: Can we use Woolworths or Seedlab Australia logos on any of our documents?
Answer: No. These are their and our logos and both companies take a dim view of other people using them
loosely.
Hint: Woolworths also don’t like being referred to as “Woollies” or “Woolies” – despite what they say in their
own TV ads.


Question: How do I keep frozen samples frozen (or chilled samples chilled) on my stand?
Answer: Suggest you use a small number of samples on a bowl or tub with ice – and either our or your team
can replenish from fridges or freezers in the Woolworths preparation kitchens.


Question: What is the floor area for my stand?
Answer: It will differ depending on where you are on the floorplan – please itemise your requirements with
specifics, to Hazel, if they are critical to your display planning.

9th June 2023 Update:-

Remember: we have asked to see a mock up display of your table so we can accommodate you best on the floorplan. Sooner rather than later please. i.e. NOW.

Question: How ahead in time do we need to send the samples? Will they store it for us in the Cool room and Freezer?

Answer: We suggest you send ahead so that they arrive 1 week prior. Label deliveries carefully to ensure correct storage is undertaken. Please read the notes from Woolworths about this on the Module.

Question: We would require Fridge and Freezer at the table, can we get these at the tables?

Answer: Not likely. I will ask – but work on a back up plan – hiring small units, or a table display with ice. We will aim to position you as close as possible to the prep kitchen. Please be in touch.

Question: We have different products and therefore would have different Category Managers, will we be meeting all of them?

Answer: Unlikely – there is usually just one meeting, but I would prepare for on-table meetings with others so be clear on whom you are looking out for.

Question: We have different review dates (Partner Hub) – some are prior to the Expo, do we need to apply by review dates or we wait for EXPO.

Answer: DO NOT WAIT FOR THE EXPO – APPLY FOR REVIEW DATES ASAP.

Question: Is there parking?

Answer: Yes – free parking (including disabled parking), underneath the building we are actually going to be in (Woolworths Head Office), and accessed by a lift to reception, from the car park. Bring a trolley. I will ensure we know how to identify the access point for the car park (it’s to the left of the main building and down a ramp).

Note: Re correct unit abbreviations (on pack).  Suggest checking with this document: https://www.industry.gov.au/publications/guide-sale-pre-packaged-goods

23rd June 2023 Update:-

Delivery Dockets are ONLY for if you are sending stock/materials through well ahead of the day – most of you will be bringing things in by hand or on a trolley and don’t need to worry about them at all.

18th January 2024 Update:

Remember: we have asked to see a mock up display of your table so we can accommodate you best on the
floorplan. Sooner rather than later please. i.e. NOW.

Question: Preparation Time – when do we get to access and what time do we start sampling/or do people start
coming?

Answer: You can access from 8.30am on the day (be getting ready by 8am please), and we would advise your
table is ready for sampling by 11am – although official sampling is only between 12 and 2pm. Remember that
between 9am and 12pm and 2-3pm could be Category Review Meetings – there will be a half hour window in
that time when you have your meeting. This is why we have recommended you bring two people. If you are
unable to, please advise us ahead of time so we know your stand may need coverage.

Question: How ahead in time do we need to send the samples? Will they store it for us in the Cool room and
Freezer?

Answer: We suggest you send ahead so that they arrive 1 week prior. Label deliveries carefully to ensure
correct storage is undertaken. Please read the notes from Woolworths about this on the Module.

IMPORTANT: Ensure the delivery address delivered to specifically says: 1 Woolworths Way. NOT Norwest Circa
(there is a Woolworths store nearby which can cause delivery drivers some confusion.

Question: We would require Fridge and Freezer at the table, can we get these at the tables?

Answer: No - work on hiring small units, or a table display with ice. We will aim to position you as close as
possible to the prep kitchen. Please be in touch.

Question: We have different products and therefore would have different Category Managers, will we be
meeting all of them?

Answer: Unlikely – there is usually just one meeting, but I would prepare for on-table meetings with others so
be clear on whom you are looking out for.

Question: We have different review dates (Partner Hub) – some are prior to the Expo, do we need to apply by
review dates or we wait for EXPO.

Answer: DO NOT WAIT FOR THE EXPO – APPLY FOR REVIEW DATES ASAP. If you don’t know when this is – check on Partner Hub. If you don’t know who your Catgeory Manager is – please reach out.

Question: Is there parking?

Answer: Yes – free parking (including disabled parking), underneath the building we are actually going to be in (Woolworths Head Office),
and accessed by a lift to reception, from the car park. Bring a trolley. I will ensure we know how to identify the
access point for the car park (it’s to the left of the main building and down a ramp).

Question: Can I play my executive summary on a loop on a laptop with the ‘How to use my product” video and
do I send this to you first for approval?

Answer: Yes you have to send anything playing on a loop for me to approve – or rather Woolworths to approve.

Question: Can you please also let me know where the 10 slide template can be found that you provided is it on
the academy or in an email.

Answer: There is no template – the suggestions were in the initial Briefing notes which are on the Academy in
the EXPO module.

Question: Would you please remind me how to find out who my Category Manager is and their contact
information.

Answer: Normally one would look on Partner Hub – but not all CMs are listed – happy for you to ask Hazel –
being very clear on which category you are talking about.

Question: Sydney UpStores - How can I find out where the Up-Stores are in Sydney?

Answer: Up Stores in Sydney is a closely guarded secret – no one has ever been able to get a definitive list.
Double Bay, Crows Nest are good starting points.

Note: Re correct unit abbreviations (on pack). Suggest checking with this document:
https://www.industry.gov.au/publications/guide-sale-pre-packaged-goods

1st February 2024 update


Deadline is tmro for all DOCS to Hazel. Including a pic of a mock up display of your table so we can accommodate you best on the floorplan. And Power requirements.
Note: re Delivery Dockets: Change of info re Delivery Dockets – IF YOU NEED TO DO THESE - please send these to Hazel by COB Friday 2nd Feb too.
Woolworths will not be notifying you when your stock arrives in the dock. The team at the dock will accept all deliveries and keep these until your event. It will be each supplier's responsibility to track your shipment independently and ensure it's been delivered and received.

IMPORTANT: ETCH Sparkling advise – ensure you get pics showing the WHOLE delivery has been received!

IMPORTANT: Ensure the delivery address delivered to specifically says: 1 Woolworths Way. NOT
Norwest Circa (there is a Woolworths store nearby which can cause delivery drivers some confusion.

Question: Do we need a separate Allergen statement for any condiments or accessories that we use on the day?

Answer: Incorporate these into the Allergen Declaration Statement that will be on your table. Aim to consider the things you sample with and their Allergens – use Gluten Free and Non-Dairy where possible.

Question: If some of our products contain sesame or nuts OTHER than peanuts (which are banned totally from the site) – can we sample these?

Answer: Yes – BUT you have to upload by the 2nd Feb your Product Information Form containing Ingredients and Allergens info AND have the appropriate Allergen Declaration Statement on the table. No PEANUTS!

Question: Do we need to upload for approval our Business and Product Info Pack by the 2nd Feb?

Answer: No – but you DO need to be working on this and presenting it at the prep sessions so we can ensure you are on the ball for the day.

Question: How do we get refrigerated or frozen samples to the Senior Executives for the requested 10 samples?

Answer: This is very complicated as the Senior Execs do not necessarily receive their Seedlab bags on the day, the bags may sit for some time in their office. Please ask any Senior Execs or other CMs as they visit, if they would like you to arrange for a sample of your product to be made available for them at the end of the day (or take it with them now), and then liaise with Darren and Amanda on the day. You will need to have your own
cooler bags and ice blocks for this.

Question: We are presenting 12 products – do we put all of these in the Seedlab bags for Senior Executives?

Answer: NO! Please put your best 2-3 products forward for these Seedlab bags only. You take all your products
and any relevant NPD to your CM meeting in a unique bag for your CM and ACM. Along with a way to sample
them in the meeting.

Question: Do we put all our samples in a separate bag with brochures etc and hand them to Amanda for the Seedlab bags for Senior Executives?

Answer: NO! Please provide the products loose and a few brochures if necessary. No separate bags. Thanks.

Title: Distribution Channel Strategy
Content:
Determining the best distribution channel to market is an important undertaking for each and every SKU in your range. In this module Sally Fairless considers some of the channels available, their differences and how to utilise them.

Key Words: Channel, distribution, propagate

Title: The Jobs To Be Done
Content:
In this module Sally Fairless considers consumer motivation through the lens of ‘what job do they want done?’ which is an important part of the identification of the required benefits. Understanding the required benefit allows much deeper thinking in the product solution that you provide and how you can communicate your solution to the market. The presentation slides and a ‘Jobs to be Done’ worksheet can be found under the ‘Materials’ tab.

Key Words: Benefits, value proposition,consumer need, propagate

Title: The Competitor Landscape & Matrix
Content:
In this module Sally Fairless discusses techniques for evaluating the competitor landscape for your products and ways to capture the consumer value. A worksheet template for the competitor landscape can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Key Words: Competitor analysis, structured comparison, value, propagate

Title: Winning With The Customer (Consumer)
Content:
Without customers you don’t have a business. Understanding your customers (and consumers) is an essential part of ensuring that your business has a sustainable future. In this video Sally Fairless discusses some techniques for gaining this understanding, including a process known as ‘Empathy Mapping’. The associated slides and worksheets can be found under the ‘Materials’ tab.

Key Words: Customer, empathy map, experience, market segmentation, demographics

Title: Marketing That Drives Sales
Content:
In this session Sally Fairless provides some insights as to how to use marketing to drive sales based on first hand practical experience. Developing the right strategy and having the correct objectives are key. The accompanying slides and useful background links can be found under the ‘Materials’ tab.

In this second video Sally presents some case studies to help demonstrate the principles discussed.

Key words: Sally Fairless, marketing, sales, painpoint, differentiate, friction, AARRR, Unique Value Proposition, propagate

Title: How To Say It Simply
Content:
Being able to describe your company, your brand or your product in just a few words is a key skill that will help you to craft pitches, media copy and labeling that really deliver. In this module Sally Fairless, Dr Hazel MacTavish-West and Amanda Hinds introduce a practical framework that can help you create a memorable and compelling message using only a few words. The accompanying worksheets can be found under the ‘Materials’ tab.

Key Words: Saying it simply, communications, propagate

Title: Retail Marketing - Practical Approaches
Content:
In this video, Sally Fairless talks to Woolworths Executives Serena Anson-Cope and Jennifer Welsh, about how brands can stop consumers in the aisle, and get them to try new products, and to buy again. This video was presented as part of the Retail Marketing Masterclass on the 2nd November 2022.

Key Words: retail, marketing, masterclass, woolworths, cartology, Masterfoods, spice tailor, Macro organics, propagate

Title: Integrated Marketing Campaigns
Content:
In this module Sally Fairless discusses the importance of taking an integrated approach to your marketing activities to leverage effort, being consistent over the various channels of marketing campaigns and evolving to your customer's needs and they evolve.

Key Words: Marketing campaign, social media, channel, consistent, message, propagate

Title: Digital Strategy
Content:
Having a well designed strategy for your digital marketing is crucial to maximising return on investment. You must understand your audience, know where to find them, how to provide meaningful value through the campaign and to keep the message consistent.

Key Words: Digital, marketing, social media, channel, consistent, message, propagate

Title: Mentors, Advisors, Consultants & Boards
Content:
In this module Ray Butcher discusses the importance of accessing the correct sources of advice for your business. This advice can be found in many places and through numerous formal or informal methods, each with benefits and implications for your business. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Key Words: Business support, board, advisory, mentor, consultant, structure, meeting, propagate

Title: Production, processing & documentation
Content:
In this short module Ray Butcher describes a process of creating the suite of supporting documentation required for leamrepeatable and efficient production processing and how this integrates with the overal process flow model. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Key Words: Process, docuemntation, lean, gemba, kanban, flow, propagate

Title: The Management of Risk
Content:
The management of risk is one of the most important functions of a business leader (or leadership team). All operations involve risk of one form or another, and not always in obvious ways. The management of risk involves systematically identifying, assessing and instigating mitigation for the identified risks. The accompanying slides, along with a sample risk management template can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Key Words: Risk management, severity, assess, mitigate

Title: Creating a Strategic Plan
Content:
As businesses grow there will come a time when business planning needs to move beyond the operational day-to-day planning and to start to map out a strategic view of the future 3, 5, 10 years or even longer. This strategic plan can help capture this future view, along with tangible metrics, to help ensure that the business remains on target. The strategic plan is also essential for communicating the overall plan when the business may have many functional silos, each of which will have their own operation (and possibly strategic) plans that support the overarching strategy. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Key Words: Strategy, strategic, plan, goals, objective, mission, vision

Title: Establishing Trust
Content:
As a business leader, establishing trust with your team and employees is a critical aspect of developing a healthy and productive working relationship. In this short video Amanda Hinds discusses how you can approach developing this trust, based on the learnings of industry leaders in this field. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Key Words: Trust, brave, Brene Brown, Dare to Lead, Kim Scott, Radical Candor, team, communicate

Title: Performance Management
Content:
People are often one of the biggest investments that you will make in your business. As a resource, your people do require support and direction which can sometimes lead to uncomfortable conversations when the subject of performance (or lack of it) are encountered. In this short module Amanda Hinds presents some techniques that can be used to support and direct your people, both when things are going well and when those difficult conversations may be required. The accompanying slides can be fund under the 'Materials' tab.

Key Words: Performance management, structured conversation, under performing, clarity, praise, crtiticism

Title: Costs & Setting a Sell Price or RRP
Content:
Setting your sell price and recommended retail price (RRP) requires a detailed understanding of your costs if a profit is to be generated. Without understanding your costs any price set is essentially a 'best guess' which is a highly risky approach. In this short module Ray Butcher describes the processes of understanding your costs, how to derive a meaningful sell price (and RRP) and then how that information can be used to forecast business performance over the next few weeks, months and year to ensure sufficient cashflow and to highlight deviations from plans and sales forecasts. The accompanying slides and a forecast planning template can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Key Words: Cost, recommended retail price, RRP, sell price, forecast, cashflow, actual, planned, deviation

Title: Managing Your Range
Content:
Managing your range, or range rationalisation, is an important part of your product planning process. Each SKU will contribute differently to your range mix both in terms of sales volume and profit and it is important to ensure that you run with the optimum range to meet your business goals. Markets change and evolve so this is a periodic (or continuous) process required to prevent opportunity loss due to the incorrect product 'mix'. As a business process this is also important to inform range conversations that may take place with retailers and stockists. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Key Words: Range, management, pareto principle, 80, 20 rule, forecast

Title: Leadership in Action
Content:
In this module Amanda Hinds and Sally Fairless share some experiences about how to lead effectively and create high performing teams. the accompanying slides can be found under the ‘Materials’ tab.

Key words: Leadership, Brene Brown, vulnerability, shame

Title: Human Resource Management
Content:
Amanda Hinds discusses some of the aspects of Human Resource Management that need to be considered as you recruit staff and expand your business. While it can be a big step, with careful planning and execution it can be achieved with the minimum of dramas. The accompanying slides can be found under the ‘Materials’ tab.

Key Words: Amanda Hinds, Human Resource Management, recruiting, employment, staff, harassment, discrimination, performance, induction, safety

Title: Understanding Personality Types
Content:
With roots in the theories developed by Myers-Briggs the 16 personality types are based on a collection of congnitive functions which impact all aspects of life through how we process information and how we make decisions.

Having an understanding of where your personality type sits within this framework can be a useful starting point for being more aware of how you operate and how you interact with others around you, and can help you develop better strategies for for approaching challenges both in business and personal life.

There are several on-line resources that can be used to take the 16 personality test, including this site (please note, the link will take you to an external site):- 16personalities.com.

Amanda Hinds describes a little more about the 16 personalities in the video below, and the accompanying slides can be found under the ‘Materials’ tab.

Key Words: Pesonality types, 16 personalities, Myers-Briggs

Title: Funding For Promotions
Content:
Promotions are a key part of the sales strategy for retail and require careful planning. They also come at a cost, so as part of the planning phase the promotional planes must be evaluated against return on investment to ensure efficient use of cashflow (and profit). In this short module Ray Butcher considers some of the issues around funding promotions and the questions that need to be asked. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Key Words: Promotion, funding, channel, profit, revenue, cost, discount, forecast, return on investment, ROI

Title: Inventory Management and Control
Content:
In your production process having the right components or ingredients in the right place at the right time is key to efficient operation and being able to deliver to your commitments. Inventory management and control is the discipline that will help ensure that risks associated with holding stock, lead times, stock shelf life and wastage are mitigated as far as is possible by applying rigorous processes to the activity. In this module introduced by Ray Butcher we consider the importance of this function and provide some suggestions about how to approach it. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Key Words: Inventory management, control, stock, rotation, LEAN, just in time, JIT, kanban, process

Title: Trade Partner Plan TPP- Plan On A Page
Content:
The Trade Partner Plan (TPP) is a strategic plan developed between a supplier and their Woolworths Category Manager. It is a simple 'plan on a page' that captures objectives classified in 'pillars' which are broken down into actions and outcomes to achieve the overall goal, which may be as simple as to grow revenue from X to Y by Z or to increase market share.

A template for the Seedlab Australia version of this Trade Partner Plan can be found under the 'Materials' tab. Please note that this template is an example, the pillars that you will use in your own plan will be specific to your business and objectives. The second sheet of the plan can be used to capture action details that are identified from the higher level TPP.

Title: Note on Sustainability Claims
Content:
The area of sustainability claims is becoming a focus area in Australian consumer law and the notes below, provided by Paul Kelly - Head of Technical: Regulatory Compliance (Food/FNF) from Woolworths provides some general guidance about the current view of best practice when making sustainability claims, partially in the area of compostability and biodegradability. It is anticipated that the ACCC will continue their focus on ‘greenwashing’ in 2024.

We would recommend that you review and are familiar with the ACCC Environmental claims guide (external link), in particular we suggest that you refer to the following: 

  • ‘Principle 2: Have evidence to back up your claims’ in the ACCC guide pages 13-15
  • Information on ‘considering the full lifecycle of your product’ pages 17-18
  • We would like to draw your attention to the compostable example in the ACCC guideline on page 15 regarding claims that are likely to be false or misleading. The ACCC advises that ‘Good practice would be to provide further information on the scheme and outline any steps the consumer needs to take for the items to be composted’ . This example highlights the needs for special actions or limitations to the level that any claims can be made to your product (i.e. if only certain parts are compostable).

We would also recommend that you review and are familiar with the AANA Environmental Claims Code (external link): 

  • ‘SECTION 3 SUBSTANTIATION (a) shall be able to be substantiated and verifiable. Supporting information shall include sufficient detail to allow evaluation of a claim’ of the AANA Environmental Claims Code.

(Note - there is a draft update to the AANA code, which can be viewed here - external link)

Some suggestions of recognised standards/schemes that can be used to assess the compostability/biodegradability of your product (note this list is not exhaustive) are: 

  • AS 5810:2010 - Home Composting 
  • AS 4736:2006 - Industrial Composting
  • ASTM D6400
  • EN 13432
  • OK Compost (TUV)

•Consumers are willing to pay for

•Captures ALL your costs

•Competitive in your category

•Profitable margins for you!

The video pre-watch for the workshop is below and under the 'Materials' tab you will find the required worksheet and a presentation slides.

Please note: The workshop is an educational activity intended to help you better understand your business. Content:
The Pricing & Margins module is intended to help participants better understand their own cost models and where their product sits in the competitive landscape. The objectives for the workshop are that each business has a sustainable pricing model that:

•Consumers are willing to pay for

•Captures ALL your costs

•Competitive in your category

•Profitable margins for you!

The video is below and under the 'Materials' tab you will find the required worksheet and a presentation slides.

Please note: The workshop is an educational activity intended to help you better understand your business. We do not make any recommendations as to how you set your pricing, we are providing priciples and framework only. You and your trusted accountant are required to perform your calculations and ulitmately only you can decide on what is best for your busness.

Title: Writing Product Specifications
Content:
In this session, Amanda Evans-Lara takes you through best practice in developing and writing product specifications. This session was recorded in December 2025.

Using your own system (i.e. there is no prescribed format for specifications) document the specifications for your finished product (what's INSIDE the pack). Think about sensory factors (colour, smell, consistency). We'll discuss these in the live discussion. Note: this will eventually need to include packaging, labelling, packing into shelf-ready shippers etc - and each will require a separate specification sheet. Title: People and Personalities
Content:
With roots in the theories developed by Myers-Briggs the 16 personality types are based on a collection of congnitive functions which impact all aspects of life through how we process information and how we make decisions.

Having an understanding of where your personality type sits within this framework can be a useful starting point for being more aware of how you operate and how you interact with others around you, and can help you develop better strategies for for approaching challenges both in business and personal life.

There are several on-line resources that can be used to take the 16 personality test, including this site (please note, the link will take you to an external site):- 16personalities.com.

Amanda Hinds describes a little more about the 16 personalities in the video below, and the accompanying slides can be found under the ‘Materials’ tab.

Please take 10 minutes to do a personality test at https://www.16personalities.com/ and come prepared to share your learnings from this (even if you've done things like this before)

Title: The Seedlab Business Planner
Content:
The Seedlab Business Planner is a version of the popular LEAN startup plan used to guide and inform startup businesses around the world. The lessons presented throughout the Seedlab Australia program will take you through the key areas of the plan and offer instruction as to how to populate your business plan on a page.

In this video Dr Hazel MacTavish-West introduces the planner and describes how we use it. Various formats of the planner template can be found under the 'Materials' tab for download.

https://vimeo.com/1097251271/7a92069217?share=copy

The planner is designed to be printed out and used as a live document, often using sticky notes to allow rapid changes and thought experiments. A possible alternative to using a hard copy is to use an image of the planner as a background in your favourite virtual whiteboard or planning application.

Below is an example of such a plan being used in Jamboard which is a Google suite application available online (jamboard.google.com) but many other applications could be used (e.g. Trello, Miro etc.).

The Seedlab Business Planner is adapted from The Business Model Canvas and the Lean Canvas created by Ash Maurya and is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-Share Alike 3.0 Un-ported License.

Further Learning

Congratulations! By now, you should (hopefully) have your Seedlab Business Startup Plan completed (as in filled in, remember it is a living document that is never totally complete).

With this one page document you can now encapsulate the key points important to defining, describing and communicating your business both internally and to others. However, in some instances this high level detail will require expansion with further information, such as when trying to attract investment, partners or collaborators, and it is at this point the full business plan becomes useful.

For many startups creating a full business plan can seem a daunting task, but you are lucky in that you have already done a lot of the hard work in generating your startup plan. Armed with this document, and a number of other resources given below the creation of a full business plan should be no more than a ‘fill in the blanks’ exercise with information you have previously documented.

The federal government provide a range of information and tutorials, these resources are available through the portal business.gov.au and in particular for business plans this LINK (link checked 18 February 2026) where a step-by-step process is provided to guide the user through the business plan template that can be downloaded at the bottom of the page.

Title: Module 1 - How, What and When?

Title: Innovation & NPD - An Introduction
Content:
Innovation is not just product line extension. Innovation is creating a change that adds value to your business and can include new categories, markets, products, services or even new business models.

Key Words: Innovation, NPD, value, disruption, incremental, novelty, propagate, return on investment, ROI

Title: Funding Innovation
Content:
A key part of the innovation process is the planning phase. Innovation has a cost and requires a business case to justify the funding investment and a formal evaluation process. This is explored further in this short video introduced by Ray Butcher. The accompanying slides can be found under the ‘Materials’ tab.

Key Words: Innovation, NPD, value, disruption, incremental, novelty, propagate, return on investment, ROI

Title: Financial Business Cases for Innovation
Content:
Determining a solid financial business case for any investment in NPD or innovation is essential in ensuring data-based decision making and predicting return on investment so that success can be evaluated. In this module Jeremy Khoh presents a financial approach to creating such a business case to assist with this robust decision making. A worksheet template can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Key Words: Innovation, NPD, value, disruption, incremental, novelty, propagate, return on investment, ROI, business case, cannibalisation

Title: Module 1 - Overview
Content:
Helen Banyard and James Easley from ‘The Good Stuff’ introduce the ‘Stop Yelling, Start Selling’ module which has the objectives of reflecting on your strategic foundations, acquiring and retaining your best customers, creating a structured communications and messaging campaign and ultimately optimising your marketing spend.  Helen leads you through their proprietary workflow to help you undertake this process yourself reducing the need to retain expensive external resources to develop a robust marketing plan for your business and products.

Key Words: Marketing, selling, brand, The Good Stuff, Helen Banyard, Miro, messaging, audience, campaign

Title: Module 2 - Strategic Audit: Nine in 90
Content:
In this module Helen Banyard from ‘The Good Stuff’ details the proprietary tool that they use to perform a strategic audit of your current situation, called the ‘Nine in 90’.

The objective of this audit is to gain a view of your current brand and marketing situation, evaluate the strength of the foundation, highlight any gaps and help you develop concepts to make your brand compelling.  The ultimate objective is to give you confidence in your plans before committing significant resources or spend.

Worksheets and Miro links can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Key Words: Strategy, strategic, The Good Stuff, Helen Banyard, Miro, brand, marketing, audience, voice

Title: Module 3 - Audience Research
Content:
In this module Helen Banyard from ‘The Good Stuff’ describes the importance of really understanding your audience in a very specific way rather than a general population.  With this understanding you are best able to understand their problems and direct your messaging to their particular needs.

Worksheets can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Key Words: Marketing, brand, audience, persona, Helen Banyard, The Good Stuff, survey, research

Title: Module 4 - Value Proposition and Brand Positioning
Content:
Following on from audience research in the previous module Helen from ‘The Good Stuff’ introduces the ideas of the value proposition and brand positioning and their importance in communication with your customers.  The module then describes some tools that allow you explore and create these concepts for your own brand, business and products.

Worksheets and Miro links can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Key Words: Brand, marketing, position, value proposition, perception map

Title: Module 5 - Campaign
Content:
In the final module of the series, Helen Banyard from ‘The Good Stuff’ pulls together the outputs from the previous modules to generate an overall plan for your marketing campaign.  While the tools in previous modules will allow you to undertake most of the work yourselves it is often at the final campaign stage where you will decide to seek out expert professional help.  This module helps in the decision making of process around when, and how, to hand off the campaign to a professional agency, understanding what your own role should still be and how to evaluate the work generated by an external agent.

Worksheets and Miro links can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Key Words: Marketing, agency, campaign, Helen Banyard, The Good Stuff, brief

Content:
In this, the first of four sessions from Kantar, Jess Heffernan presents her view of the current trends in FMCG (particularly food) convenience. Jess has been with Kantar’s National Sensory team for 2 years and brings over a decade of experience from across the FMCG, government and supermarket industries. Beginning her career in Product Development at PepsiCo, and then moving onto dedicated sensory and consumer research at CSIRO and then Woolworths Group, Jess has experience in leading the end-to-end execution of sensory- and consumer-based projects for the broader food industry. She has a passion for all things convenience, especially relating to meal solutions. This session was recorded on the 2nd July 2024.

Title: Value with Joseph Donovan
Content:
In this, the second of four sessions from Kantar, Joseph (Joey) Donovan presents his view of the current trends in FMCG (particularly food) around the actuality and perception of value. Joey is an Associate Director at Kantar Insights. This session was recorded on the 9th July 2024. The accompanying slides can be found under the 'Materials' tab.

Some questions were taken on notice in the session and Joey has responded with these answers:-

  • What exactly is the reduction in "paying more for sustainability" related to": I think this is a hard one to tease out and it definitely is influenced by cost-of-living pressures being faced by everyone. However, I also looked into a stat from out GlobalMonitor data and found there is a higher proportion of GenZ (58%) who feel businesses/governments are more responsible for fixing environmental problems than themselves compared to younger millennials (50%) and especially middle age (43%). I don't have information on how this has shifted in the past few years though. However, today is seems there is a higher expectation that businesses should just naturally be doing good with the younger generation. 
  • Trends in personal care packaging: I loved this question, but I was fresh out of ideas. I did think of some other trends though over the last few days I have noticed
    • Packaging that limits waste: Solving a pain point of some personal care packaging (like bottles, squeezy tubes, rigid packaging with small openings, etc.) that always leave you with throwing some of the product out. People don't want to see things go to waste. I think this is where novel pump ideas (like HiSmile) or packaging with removeable interiors (think lotion where you can replace the inside container that holds the cream) come into play. From the frame of value, this is allowing a consumer to get more for their money (reduction in waste, but with a personal benefit, like the Woolies "Odd Bunch" example)
    • Mini/Travel: I have seen the mini space pick-up in this industry. You no longer need to use a B-grade product when you travel because that is all that's available in a small size. Like the Mini/Maxi trend we discussed, companies are making smaller sizes of their products available for when you are on the go. Mecca has a whole portion of their website dedicated to mini/travel!
    • Reuseable/Refillable: Again, a sustainability play, but it adds value for the consumer because refilling costs less. One brand I know that does this is Le Labo (you will have to go to the US website) but I am sure there are tons of others. I also think Sephora has a sustainability program with some cosmetics. (Note links to external sites provided).

Key Words: Trends, 2024, Kantar, insights, data, value, solutions, perception
